answer:
**Johann Gottlieb Fichte's *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens* (1793) centrally treats freedom of thought (*Denkfreiheit*) as its core topic.**[^1] This Enlightenment-era political pamphlet, subtitled *Reclaiming Freedom of Thought from the Princes of Europe, Who Have Hitherto Suppressed It*, consists of two chapters that argue princes have no right to restrict thought, communication of ideas, or inquiry, as these are inalienable human rights derived from the moral law and personhood.[^2] Fichte contends that freedom of thought is essential to humanity's progress, cannot be alienated in the social contract, and that suppressing it leads to stagnation or violent revolution; he urges princes to protect rights rather than play god over truth.[^3]

Freedom of thought appears more incidentally in other works, often linked to religion, press freedom, or slavery:

- Charles Comte's *Traité de Législation, Vol. III* (Ch. 28) observes that complete freedom of religious opinion in China implies limited constraints on thought outside government matters, with no pre-publication restrictions.[^4]
- The same author's *Traité de Législation, Vol. IV* (Ch. 15) discusses how Southern U.S. slaveholders ban literacy to prevent "freedom of thought" from instructing slaves, as it would undermine servitude amid press freedoms enjoyed by masters.[^5]

Related discussions of censorship (as a threat to thought expression) appear in Comte's *Traité de Législation, Vol. I* (Chs. 20–21),[^6] *Traité de la Propriété* (Vols. I–II),[^7] and Charles Dunoyer's *Nouveau Traité d'Économie* (Vols. I–II),[^8] but these do not focus on freedom of thought per se.

No other works in the 37-work corpus treat it as a sustained topic.[^9]

[^1]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, metadata description: "Early political pamphlet on freedom of thought by Johann Gottlieb Fichte" (/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/metadata/).

[^2]: Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit*, Ch. 1 (/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/01/translation/#p-effd89); Ch. 2 (/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-be3fae).

[^3]: Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit*, Ch. 1, [p-47d2fc](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/01/translation/#p-47d2fc), [p-aa8790](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/01/translation/#p-aa8790); Ch. 2, [p-8e054b](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-8e054b), [p-c1eedc](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-c1eedc), [p-44e1de](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02/translation/#p-44e1de).

[^4]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation, Vol. III*, Ch. 28, [p-2ea79e](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/28-des-rapports-observes-entre-les-moyens-d-existence/translation/#p-2ea79e).

[^5]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation, Vol. IV*, Ch. 15, [p-5096be](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iv-bca040/15-de-l-influence-de-l-esclavage-domestique-sur-l-esp/translation/#p-5096be).

[^6]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation, Vol. I*, Ch. 20, [p-55e535](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/20-de-la-puissance-qui-appartient-a-chacun-des-elemen/translation/#p-55e535); Ch. 21, [p-4ddd7c](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/21-de-l-action-des-lois-de-la-morale-et-des-obstacles/translation/#p-4ddd7c).

[^7]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la Propriété, Vol. I*, Preface, [p-ff8e10](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/00-preface/translation/#p-ff8e10); *Vol. II*, Ch. 33, [p-e135d2](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/33-des-lois-relatives-a-la-propriete-des-compositions/translation/#p-e135d2).

[^8]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau Traité d'Économie, Vol. I*, Ch. 7, [p-ffe222](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/07-ii-liberte-compatible-avec-la-maniere-de-vivre-des/translation/#p-ffe222); *Vol. II*, Ch. 19, [p-2f42c1](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/19-ix-de-la-liberte-des-arts-qui-travaillent-a-leduca/translation/#p-2f42c1).

[^9]: Derived from `list_works` (37 total) and exhaustive `search_corpus` on "freedom of thought" (16 hits, all traced above) and synonyms/variants (0–10 hits, mostly tangential).
